description_long: This object is a classic revolver-style firearm with a long, cylindrical barrel and a prominent rotating cylinder for ammunition. The frame and barrel are constructed from dark metal, with visible screws and mechanical details along the body. The grip is made of a dark, polished wood, ergonomically shaped for comfortable handling. The trigger guard is rounded and metallic, enclosing a curved trigger. The hammer and loading mechanism are visible above the grip, and the barrel features a tubular under-barrel component, likely for structural reinforcement or loading. The overall design is reminiscent of 19th-century percussion cap revolvers, with a robust and utilitarian appearance.
description: A dark metal revolver with a long barrel, wooden grip, and visible rotating cylinder, featuring classic mechanical details and a rounded trigger guard.
description_view_0: Front view showing the circular barrel opening and the symmetrical alignment of the under-barrel tube.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the full length of the barrel, the wooden grip, the rotating cylinder, and the trigger guard.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the back of the wooden grip, the hammer, and the top of the cylinder.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the barrel, grip, and mechanical details from the left side.
attribute_barrel_configuration: single-barrel, long cylindrical barrel with an under-barrel tube
attribute_trigger_guard_design: rounded, medium-sized metallic guard with smooth edges and no finger grooves
